$description: SRF Botanical Glyph Catalog. 16 distinct lotus illustrations — 15 extracted from yogananda.org, 1 from the SRF registration mark — optimized with SVGO 4.0, converted to fill="currentColor" for theme-responsive rendering. Each glyph is a unique botanical form — together they compose a visual alphabet for ornamental typography.

collection.format: SVG with fill="currentColor" — no hardcoded colors
collection.optimization: SVGO 4.0.0 default preset + post-processed to remove inline style attributes
collection.original-naming: Sequential lotus-{01–16}. Renumbered from sparse original set with gaps. First wave (01–12): 11 from initial extraction + 1 promoted registration mark. Second wave (13–16): 4 from deeper site archaeology.
collection.css-delivery: CSS mask-image (primary) or inline SVG with currentColor (component)

lotus-01.$description: Compact lotus bloom. Nearly square proportion — versatile for ornamental use at any size.

lotus-02.$description: Broad landscape lotus. The most richly detailed glyph — largest file size in the collection.

lotus-03.$description: Full square bloom with high detail. Second-largest glyph.

lotus-04.$description: Wide landscape lotus. Strong horizontal emphasis for section dividers.

lotus-05.$description: Ultra-wide banner lotus — the most horizontal glyph at nearly 3:1 ratio. Lightest by file size. Ideal for section dividers and horizontal rules.

lotus-06.$description: Wide banner lotus at 2.4:1 ratio. Clean lines, moderate detail.

lotus-07.$description: Moderate landscape lotus. Balanced between ornamental and horizontal use.

lotus-08.$description: Wide banner lotus at 2:1 ratio. Clean and moderately detailed.

lotus-09.$description: Compact square lotus. Smallest footprint in the square group.

lotus-10.$description: The only portrait-oriented glyph — taller than wide (0.91:1). Unique vertical presence. Suited for focal points and contemplative compositions.

lotus-11.$description: Nearly perfect square (1.02:1). The most balanced glyph — works at any size.

lotus-12.$description: Elaborate floral registration mark promoted from icons. Highly ornamental, dense botanical detail. Nearly square (1.21:1).

lotus-13.$description: Portrait lotus (0.89:1). Second portrait form — joins lotus-10 as the only taller-than-wide glyphs. Sourced from site lotus-2_orange_dark.svg.

lotus-14.$description: Landscape lotus (1.29:1). Moderate horizontal emphasis. Sourced from site lotus-11_black_light.svg (CMS label: Lighthouse - Asset 2).

lotus-15.$description: Landscape lotus (1.54:1). Clean horizontal form. Sourced from site lotus-13_orange_light.svg.

lotus-16.$description: Landscape lotus (1.35:1). Originally yellow-gold coloring. Sourced from site lotus-17_yellow_dark.svg (CMS label: Asset 15).

audio.$description: Small audio indicator icon. 11x11 viewBox, minimal detail.

banner.$description: Ultra-wide horizontal forms. Natural section dividers. Use at 48–120px wide.

landscape.$description: Moderate horizontal forms. Work as dividers or standalone ornaments.

square.$description: Balanced forms. Most versatile — ornaments, chapter endings, empty states.

portrait.$description: Taller than wide. Unique vertical presence. Hero accents, meditation focal points.

color-voices.$description: Five color voices of one devotional palette. Four discovered archaeologically from the original yogananda.org lotus SVGs; the fifth (crimson) from the AoY Kindle edition. Together they trace an Indian devotional spectrum: gold (tejas — spiritual radiance), olive-green (prakṛti — living nature), navy (nīla — meditative depth), ochre (kāṣāya — renunciant warmth), crimson (kumkum — auspicious power). Default voice is sacred (gold). Colors respond to theme — dark themes (including YSS night and devotion) get lighter values for visibility. Crimson activates only under data-publication for structural motifs (divider, close); breath motifs stay gold. CSS: css/motifs.css. Semantics: attention-gradient.language.json motif-integration.

sacred.source: SRF Gold — the primary design accent

growth.source: Original green_dark from yogananda.org lotus SVGs

devotion.source: Original blue_dark from yogananda.org lotus SVGs

ochre.source: Original orange_dark from yogananda.org lotus SVGs

crimson.source: AoY Kindle edition chapter titles and section dividers (2025)

divider.$description: Section break between content blocks. Centered, generous vertical margin. Banner or landscape glyphs preferred.

breath.$description: Lighter pause between paragraphs or thoughts. More subtle than a divider.

close.$description: Chapter ending ornament. Small, centered, final. Square glyphs preferred.
